sections.title: Autotriage of Security Findings
sections.intro: AI/LLM tools that triage, deduplicate, prioritize, or validate the output of scanners and finding sources.

entries.name: nuclei-autotriage
entries.desc: Two-stage LLM triage (falsifier + red-team pass) of Nuclei JSONL findings via OpenAI-compatible endpoints (vLLM/Ollama).
entries.note: — **note:** restrictive personal/non-commercial EULA, not a permissive OSS license.

entries.name: seclab-taskflow-agent
entries.desc: YAML-driven taskflow agent framework for triaging CodeQL/SAST alerts and filtering false positives.

entries.name: honeyslop
entries.desc: Code-canary decoys to triage AI-hallucinated ("slop") vulnerability reports flooding bug-bounty programs.

entries.name: nano-analyzer
entries.desc: Minimal three-stage LLM pipeline (context → scan → skeptical triage) for zero-day discovery in C/C++.

entries.name: SigmaOptimizer
entries.desc: Generates, tests, and refines Sigma rules from real logs with false-positive checking.

entries.name: ai-soc-triage-assistant
entries.desc: SOC alert triage assistant with prompt-injection guardrails, output validation, and MITRE ATT&CK mapping.

sections.footer: > See also: OpenAI's *Aardvark* / *Codex Security* research previews — public references exist, but there is no standalone installable repo to badge here.

sections.title: AI Agent & Coding-Agent Security
sections.intro: Securing the AI agents themselves — auditing coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw), scanning skills / plugins / MCP manifests, and governance for agentic development. A fast-moving 2026 category, split below by role.

entries.name: agent-audit
entries.desc: Forensic auditor for local AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI, OpenClaw) **and** project-surface scanner for repos shipping skills, plugins, and MCP manifests; 296 bundled rules across native + imported detector families, with optional LLM cross-verification.

entries.name: AI-Infra-Guard
entries.desc: Full-stack AI red-teaming platform covering OpenClaw security scan, agent scan, skills scan, MCP scan, AI-infra vulnerability scan, and LLM jailbreak evaluation.

entries.name: SkillSpector
entries.desc: Security scanner for AI-agent skills used by Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and similar ecosystems; combines static analysis, AST/YARA/taint checks, optional LLM semantic review, MCP least-privilege/tool-poisoning checks, risk scoring, and SARIF/JSON/Markdown output.

entries.name: Ramparts
entries.desc: Rust scanner for MCP servers and agent-skill bundles with YARA rules, optional LLM analysis, OSV/CVE lookups, OWASP MCP Top 10 mapping, and SARIF/JSON/Markdown reports.

entries.name: mcp-armor
entries.desc: Local MCP security scanner with auto-discovery for agentic IDE configs, tool/resource/prompt inventory, prompt-injection checks, rug-pull and tool-poisoning detection, baseline drift monitoring, and JSON/Markdown reports.

entries.name: aguara
entries.desc: Single-binary static scanner (Go, no LLM) for AI-agent skills and MCP servers; multi-layer engine (pattern + NLP + taint tracking + rug-pull detection). Companion **[aguara-mcp](https://github.com/garagon/aguara-mcp)** exposes scanning as an MCP tool.

entries.name: agent-scan
entries.desc: Security scanner for AI agents, MCP servers, and agent skills; the successor path for the original Invariant Labs mcp-scan work.

entries.name: inkog
entries.desc: Commercial-backed static security scanner for AI agents across LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, and no-code workflows; Apache-2.0 CLI with proprietary deep-scan engine.

entries.name: AgentShield
entries.desc: Security scanner for AI-agent configurations, MCP servers, hooks, and tool permissions with CLI, GitHub Action, and app workflows.

entries.name: repo-forensics
entries.desc: Offline scanner for AI-agent repos, skills, plugins, and MCP servers; license is PolyForm Noncommercial.

entries.name: skill-scanner
entries.desc: Scanner for agent skills combining YAML + YARA patterns, LLM-as-a-judge, and behavioral dataflow analysis (Codex / Cursor skill formats).

entries.name: mcp-scanner
entries.desc: Scanner for MCP servers and agentic tool surfaces, covering tools, prompts, resources, package risk, malware indicators, and deployment readiness.

entries.name: mcp-guardian
entries.desc: JS/TS library and CLI for detecting prompt injection in MCP tool descriptions and pinning tool definitions.

entries.name: agentic-radar
entries.desc: CLI security scanner for agentic workflows (LangGraph, CrewAI, n8n, etc.) — maps tools/data flows and flags risks.

entries.name: skilltotal
entries.desc: Offline deterministic static scanner (regex + AST, no LLM, no account) for AI components — agent skills/plugins, MCP servers, npm & PyPI packages, and git repos; flags supply-chain risk, dangerous capabilities, prompt-injection surfaces, MCP tool poisoning/shadowing, and data-exfiltration paths, maps to the OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10, and emits JSON + SARIF 2.1.0. *(skilltotal.ai)*

entries.name: asamm
entries.desc: *Agentic SAMM* — an OWASP SAMM extension for AI-driven development: an entry-point-based threat taxonomy plus 17 controls across 5 SAMM functions (Governance, Design, Implementation, Verification, Operations) with L1/L2/L3 maturity. License: CC BY-SA 4.0.

entries.name: agent-threat-rules (ATR)
entries.desc: Open, versioned, machine-readable detection-rule format for AI-agent threats (prompt injection, tool poisoning, MCP attacks, skill compromise) — "Sigma for agents" (MIT). Engine-agnostic; converts to Splunk/Elastic/SARIF.

entries.name: MCP-Security-Checklist
entries.desc: Security checklist for MCP clients, servers, multi-MCP deployments, lifecycle controls, authz/authn, isolation, and crypto-specific MCP integrations.

entries.name: Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
entries.desc: Large community cybersecurity skill library for AI agents, mapped to MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF, MITRE ATLAS, D3FEND, and NIST AI RMF.
entries.note: — **note:** independent community project, not affiliated with Anthropic.

entries.name: Claude-BugHunter
entries.desc: Claude Code / agent-skill bundle for authorized bug hunting and external red-team workflows across web, API, identity, cloud, recon, reporting, Burp MCP, slash commands, and the cbh CLI.
entries.note: — **note:** skill bundle and workflow knowledge base, not a standalone scanner.

entries.name: AgentDojo
entries.desc: Benchmark environment for prompt-injection attacks and defenses in tool-using LLM agents.

entries.name: ToolHive
entries.desc: Platform for running MCP servers in isolated containers with per-request identity/access policy, registry and gateway workflows, audit logs, Kubernetes operator support, and observability hooks.

entries.name: Pipelock
entries.desc: AI-agent firewall and verifiable egress-control layer mediating HTTP, WebSocket, CONNECT, MCP, and A2A traffic to detect prompt injection, secret exfiltration, SSRF, and suspicious outbound actions.
entries.note: — **note:** open-source core is Apache-2.0; commercial reporting/features are also advertised.

entries.name: mcp-context-protector
entries.desc: MCP security wrapper that sits in front of downstream MCP servers, scans tool responses with guardrail providers, and supports quarantine/review workflows for desktop and coding-agent MCP configs.

entries.name: MCP Defender
entries.desc: Desktop app that proxies MCP tool-call requests and responses for Cursor, Claude, VS Code, and Windsurf, checks intercepted traffic against signatures, and prompts users to allow or block suspicious calls.
entries.note: — **note:** AGPL-3.0 licensed; project has been acquired by Docker.

entries.name: MCP Gateway
entries.desc: Plugin-based MCP gateway that proxies configured MCP servers, sanitizes sensitive request/response data, supports guardrail plugins such as basic masking and Presidio, and runs a server reputation/risk check before loading MCP servers.

entries.name: Parallax
entries.desc: Rust runtime policy engine for AI agents: evaluates lifecycle events with regex, keyword, Sigma, CEL, and SQL rules to block or redact prompt injection, data exfiltration, dangerous tool calls, and secret leakage.
entries.note: — **note:** early-stage project with limited adoption signal.

entries.name: Armorer Guard
entries.desc: Local Rust scanner and MCP proxy for AI-agent prompt injection, credential leakage, exfiltration, and risky tool-call arguments, with structured reasons and no scanner network calls.
entries.note: — **note:** young project with limited independent adoption signal.

entries.name: onecli
entries.desc: Credential gateway and encrypted vault for AI agents; injects real API credentials at the gateway so agents only see placeholder keys.

entries.name: microsandbox
entries.desc: Local-first, microVM-backed programmable sandboxes for AI agents with SDKs, CLI, MCP support, and rootless hardware isolation.

entries.name: agentguard
entries.desc: Real-time security layer for coding agents: hooks scan every new skill, block dangerous actions before execution, run daily posture patrols, and track which skill triggered each action (incl. Web3-specific checks).

entries.name: defenseclaw
entries.desc: Enforcement and evidence layer for agentic deployments: static CodeGuard checks, sandboxing, registry ingestion with SSRF guards, and audit/observability.

entries.name: AgentFence
entries.desc: Runtime-oriented test harness for deployed agents, probing prompt injection, secret leakage, and system-instruction exposure.
entries.note: *Early-stage project.*

entries.name: clawsec
entries.desc: Security skill suite for OpenClaw-family agents; AGPL-3.0 licensed.

entries.name: h5i
entries.desc: Local Rust CLI for auditable coding-agent workspaces: per-agent worktrees with sandbox policies, provenance capture, peer review, neutral verification, secret/prompt-injection audit signals, and refs/h5i/* run metadata.
entries.note: — **note:** security-adjacent agent-workspace governance tool, not a vulnerability scanner or VM-equivalent sandbox.

entries.name: DvalinCode
entries.desc: Local-first AI coding agent with runtime governance controls: org/repo policy gates for tools, models, MCP servers, paths, and commands, plus provider/shell/MCP egress controls and hash-chained audit logs.
entries.note: — **note:** young project with limited independent adoption signal.

sections.title: AI/ML Supply Chain & Model Security
sections.intro: Tools for securing model artifacts, serialized ML files, AI/ML supply-chain surfaces, and malicious-package detection datasets/benchmarks.

entries.name: Fraim
entries.desc: Framework for AI-powered security workflows including LLM SAST and IaC analysis with SARIF/HTML output.

entries.name: Adversarial Robustness Toolbox (ART)
entries.desc: Flagship machine-learning security library for evaluating and defending models against evasion, poisoning, extraction, and inference attacks across major ML frameworks.

entries.name: Foolbox
entries.desc: Classic Python toolbox for generating adversarial examples and benchmarking robustness of PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX models.

entries.name: modelscan
entries.desc: Scans ML model files for unsafe serialization patterns and embedded code, with a focus on model serialization attacks.

entries.name: Fickling
entries.desc: Python pickle decompiler, rewriter, and static analyzer for inspecting and detecting malicious pickle/PyTorch payloads.

entries.name: picklescan
entries.desc: Lightweight CLI/library for detecting suspicious Python pickle operations in ML and model artifacts.

entries.name: AIsbom
entries.desc: AI software bill of materials tooling for AI/ML supply-chain inventory and provenance metadata.

entries.name: model-provenance-kit
entries.desc: Toolkit for model-family provenance and fingerprinting across model weights, tokenizers, and architecture signals.

entries.name: pickle-fuzzer
entries.desc: Structure-aware fuzzer for pickle scanners, useful for hardening tools such as modelscan, Fickling, and picklescan.

entries.name: Medusa
entries.desc: AI-first security scanner for AI/ML repos, agents, and MCP surfaces; AGPL-3.0 licensed.

entries.name: PrivacyRaven
entries.desc: Privacy-testing library for deep-learning systems, covering model extraction and membership-inference style attacks.
entries.note: — **note:** archived/hiatus project, but still a useful reference implementation.

entries.name: gym-malware
entries.desc: OpenAI Gym environment for reinforcement-learning agents that mutate PE malware to evade static ML malware detectors.

entries.name: deep-pwning
entries.desc: Historical "Metasploit for machine learning" framework for experimenting with adversarial robustness of ML models.

entries.name: open-malicious-code-benchmark
entries.desc: OMCBench benchmark suite for malicious-code/package detection: labeled Python and JavaScript package archives, common runners, and published precision/recall/F1 metrics.
entries.note: — **note:** evaluates an unreleased commercial ML detector (MOLOT / PT Application Inspector) alongside open-source baselines.

entries.name: malicious-software-packages-dataset
entries.desc: Human-vetted dataset of malicious software packages across npm, PyPI, IDE extensions, and AI Skills, useful for detector training and evaluation.
entries.note: — **note:** contains real malware samples; Datadog notes selection bias because many samples were identified by GuardDog.

entries.name: GuardDog
entries.desc: CLI for detecting malicious PyPI, npm, Go, RubyGems, GitHub Actions, and VSCode extension packages using Semgrep rules and package-metadata heuristics.

entries.name: package-analysis
entries.desc: Sandboxed static/dynamic analysis pipeline for open-source packages, capturing filesystem, process, and network behavior and publishing data for malicious-package research.

entries.name: malicious-code-ruleset
entries.desc: Focused Semgrep ruleset for malicious-code patterns such as dynamic execution and obfuscation, used as an OMCBench baseline.

entries.name: pypi_malregistry
entries.desc: ASE'23 / USENIX Security'26 malicious-PyPI dataset with more than 10k malicious package versions.
entries.note: — **note:** no LICENSE file found and the repository contains malware samples; handle in an isolated environment.

sections.title: Pentest & Red-Team Agents
sections.intro: Autonomous and semi-autonomous AI agents for penetration testing, exploitation, and attack simulation.

entries.name: PentestGPT
entries.desc: The original USENIX'24 LLM pentest agent; re-released as an autonomous pipeline with strong benchmark results.

entries.name: PentAGI
entries.desc: Fully autonomous multi-agent pentest framework with Docker sandboxing.

entries.name: CAI – Cybersecurity AI
entries.desc: Modular, bug-bounty-ready agent framework supporting 300+ LLM models. MIT for research; separate commercial license for production/on-prem.

entries.name: Strix
entries.desc: Autonomous "AI hackers" that dynamically run code and validate vulnerabilities with PoCs (Apache-2.0).

entries.name: hackingBuddyGPT
entries.desc: Minimal (~50 LOC) research framework for LLM-driven Linux priv-esc and web pentesting (FSE'23).

entries.name: Nebula
entries.desc: AI pentesting CLI assistant with local-LLM support (Llama-3.1, Mistral, DeepSeek).

entries.name: HexStrike-AI
entries.desc: MCP server exposing 150+ security tools (nmap, gobuster, nuclei, …) to AI agents (MIT).

entries.name: Burp Suite MCP Server
entries.desc: Official Burp Suite extension exposing Burp to AI clients through MCP.
entries.note: — **note:** GPL-3.0 licensed.

entries.name: pentest-ai
entries.desc: Offensive-security MCP server with 200+ wrapped tools, specialist agents, and OWASP-oriented probes for authorized testing.

entries.name: pentest-ai-agents
entries.desc: Collection of Claude Code offensive-security subagents for authorized penetration-testing research.

entries.name: DarkMoon
entries.desc: Autonomous AI penetration-testing platform that orchestrates specialized web, AD, Kubernetes, CMS, and framework agents through an MCP-controlled Docker toolbox with local privacy-tokenization for sensitive target data.
entries.note: — **note:** GPL-3.0 licensed; heavy Docker/LLM stack, use only for authorized testing.

entries.name: T3MP3ST
entries.desc: Autonomous offensive-security meta-harness that wraps local or API-backed coding agents into a multi-agent recon-to-exploit workflow with MCP/API, War Room UI, tool arsenal, and committed benchmark artifacts.
entries.note: — **note:** very new AGPL-3.0 project with bold benchmark claims; use only for authorized testing and verify independently before operational use.

entries.name: Shannon
entries.desc: White-box autonomous AI pentester with strong XBOW-benchmark results.
entries.note: *Shannon Lite is AGPL-3.0; Shannon Pro is commercial.*

entries.name: AIDA
entries.desc: Model-agnostic autonomous pentest agent running inside an isolated Docker environment; AGPL-3.0 licensed.

entries.name: HackSynth
entries.desc: Planner/summarizer LLM-agent framework for autonomous penetration testing and benchmark evaluation; AGPL-3.0 licensed.

entries.name: VulnBot
entries.desc: Multi-agent collaborative penetration-testing framework with RAG support.

entries.name: PentestAgent
entries.desc: Black-box AI pentest framework with MCP, multi-agent spawning, and persistent sessions.

entries.name: cyber-security-llm-agents
entries.desc: AutoGen-based agents for cybersecurity tasks (shown at RSAC 2024).

entries.name: Pentest-Swarm-AI
entries.desc: Swarm-intelligence multi-agent pentest with stigmergic blackboard coordination (Go).

entries.name: hackGPT
entries.desc: LLM offensive-security toolkit.

sections.title: AI-Powered Recon & Narrow ML Tools
sections.intro: Hyper-specific AI/ML tools for a single offensive-security, recon, or detection step — the subwiz/eyeballer pattern rather than broad autonomous agents. 🅐 = self-contained trained model or learned model/pattern engine; 🅑 = LLM wrapper that calls an external API.

entries.name: subwiz
entries.desc: 🅐 Lightweight nanoGPT model that predicts resolvable subdomains via beam search; model weights are published on Hugging Face.

entries.name: regulator
entries.desc: 🅐 Learns and ranks regex-like naming patterns from known subdomains to generate likely new candidates.
entries.note: — **note:** no LICENSE file found; treat as source-available until clarified.

entries.name: eyeballer
entries.desc: 🅐 Convolutional neural network that classifies pentest/recon screenshots (login pages, webapps, old-looking sites, parked domains, and custom 404s) for attack-surface triage.
entries.note: — **note:** GPL-3.0 licensed.

entries.name: GyoiThon
entries.desc: 🅐 Machine-learning-assisted web intelligence tool that fingerprints products, versions, CVEs, login pages, debug messages, and related web-server signals from HTTP responses.
entries.note: — **note:** older project; Apache-2.0 licensed, but maintenance appears low.

entries.name: http-fingerprint-transformer
entries.desc: 🅐 Transformer-encoded HTTP-header fingerprinting experiment for classifying web-server software and versions.
entries.note: — **note:** thesis artifact; code is GPL-3.0 and data is CC BY-SA 4.0.

entries.name: ffufai
entries.desc: 🅑 AI wrapper around the ffuf web fuzzer that suggests file extensions and paths from the target URL and headers using OpenAI or Anthropic models.
entries.note: — **note:** requires an LLM API key; README states MIT but no LICENSE file was found.

entries.name: PassGPT
entries.desc: 🅐 GPT-style password model trained on leaked passwords for research on password generation and strength estimation.
entries.note: Research-only / non-commercial use; related code: [javirandor/passgpt](https://github.com/javirandor/passgpt).

entries.name: PassGAN
entries.desc: 🅐 WGAN that learns password distributions from leaks to generate guesses; historical reference implementation of the PassGAN paper (MIT).

entries.name: neural_network_cracking
entries.desc: 🅐 RNN password-guessing model from *Fast, Lean, and Accurate: Modeling Password Guessability Using Neural Networks* (USENIX Security 2016); Apache-2.0 licensed.

entries.name: phishing-url-detection
entries.desc: 🅐 Packaged URL phishing classifier with ONNX and pickle artifacts.
entries.note: Model card recommends ONNX over pickle for safer inference.

entries.name: Phishing Email Detection DistilBERT v2.4.1
entries.desc: DistilBERT text-classification model for email and URL phishing detection, trained on a public Hugging Face phishing-email dataset.
entries.note: — **note:** strong download signal, but independently verify the very high published metrics before production use.

entries.name: PhishIntention
entries.desc: 🅐 Deep-vision phishing detector that infers both brand intention and credential-taking intention from webpage appearance and dynamics (USENIX Security 2022).
entries.note: — **note:** CC0-1.0 licensed.

entries.name: VisualPhishNet
entries.desc: 🅐 Triplet CNN for zero-day phishing detection by visual similarity to trusted websites (ACM CCS 2020).
entries.note: — **note:** no LICENSE file found; dataset access is research-request based.

entries.name: yaraml_rules
entries.desc: 🅐 Trains scikit-learn classifiers on malware/benign corpora and compiles the learned model into deployable YARA rules (Apache-2.0).

entries.name: DeepSQLi
entries.desc: 🅐 Deep-learning SQL-injection detector with dataset, trained models, and a Flask Prediction API for GatewayD IDS/IPS integration.
entries.note: — **note:** AGPL-3.0 licensed; defensive detector rather than offensive generator.

entries.name: deepsecrets
entries.desc: Semantic secrets scanner using lexing/parsing, entropy checks, and hashed-known-secret matching across 500+ languages.
entries.note: — **note:** useful narrow detector, but not a trained ML model.

entries.name: VLAI Vulnerability Severity Classifier
entries.desc: RoBERTa-based vulnerability-severity classifier trained on CIRCL vulnerability scores to assist triage before manual CVSS scoring.

sections.title: AI-Powered SAST & Secure Code Review
sections.intro: Static analysis and secure code review enhanced with LLMs.

entries.name: Vulnhuntr
entries.desc: Zero-shot vulnerability discovery in Python repos via LLM call-chain analysis; credited with a 0-day RCE in Ragflow.

entries.name: deepsec
entries.desc: Agent-powered security harness for scanning large codebases with coding agents, resumable parallel runs, custom matchers, and optional revalidation.

entries.name: defending-code-reference-harness
entries.desc: Reference Claude Code skills and autonomous vulnerability-discovery pipeline for threat modeling, static scanning, triage, execution-verified C/C++ memory-bug discovery, reporting, and patch generation.
entries.note: — **note:** official reference implementation, not maintained as a product; the autonomous pipeline executes target code and should be run only inside the documented gVisor sandbox.

entries.name: claude-code-security-review
entries.desc: Official Claude-based semantic SAST GitHub Action that reviews PR diffs.

entries.name: IRIS
entries.desc: Neurosymbolic SAST combining LLMs with CodeQL for Java vulnerability detection (MIT).

entries.name: sast-skills
entries.desc: Agent skills that turn AI coding assistants into a multi-agent SAST scanner.

entries.name: llm-sast-scanner
entries.desc: SAST skill for AI coding agents with structured source-to-sink analysis across 34 vulnerability classes.
entries.note: *License: MIT stated in README.*

entries.name: sast-ai-workflow
entries.desc: LangGraph workflow for reviewing static-analysis findings, reducing false positives, and producing vulnerability review output.

entries.name: xvulnhuntr
entries.desc: Archived fork of Vulnhuntr extending support to C#, Java, and Go.

entries.name: llm-security-scanner
entries.desc: LLM-powered code scanner that opens GitHub issues for findings.

entries.name: vulnhuntr-mod
entries.desc: Modified Vulnhuntr with Qwen/Hunyuan support and Chinese-language prompts.

sections.title: LLM-Driven Fuzzing
sections.intro: Two families: (a) LLMs generating harnesses/targets for traditional fuzzing, and (b) fuzzing the LLM itself.

entries.name: oss-fuzz-gen
entries.desc: LLM-driven fuzz-harness generation for OSS-Fuzz; reported 26 real vulnerabilities (incl. CVE-2024-9143 in OpenSSL).

entries.name: PromptFuzz
entries.desc: LLM-mutated prompts to generate fuzz drivers for C/C++ libraries (Rust).

entries.name: Fuzz4All
entries.desc: "Universal" LLM-based fuzzer across compilers/languages (ICSE 2024).

entries.name: ChatAFL
entries.desc: LLM-guided protocol fuzzing extending AFLNet (NDSS'24).

entries.name: TitanFuzz
entries.desc: First LLM-based fuzzer for PyTorch/TensorFlow (ISSTA'23).

entries.name: LLMFuzzer
entries.desc: First open-source fuzzing framework for LLM API integrations.

entries.name: ps-fuzz
entries.desc: System-prompt hardening fuzzer; 16 attacks × 16 providers.

entries.name: FuzzyAI
entries.desc: Automated LLM fuzzer for jailbreaks/prompt injection.

entries.name: spikee
entries.desc: Prompt-injection evaluation and exploitation kit with dataset generation, Burp integration, and pluggable judges.

entries.name: promptmap
entries.desc: Prompt-injection scanner for custom LLM applications in white-box and black-box modes; GPL-3.0 licensed.

entries.name: ai-prompt-fuzzer
entries.desc: Burp Suite extension fuzzing GenAI/LLM prompts.

sections.title: Threat Intelligence
sections.intro: AI/LLM tooling for CTI gathering, IOC/TTP extraction, and analysis.

entries.name: trs
entries.desc: LLM + ChromaDB tool to summarize threat reports and extract MITRE TTPs and IOCs.

entries.name: TI-Mindmap-GPT
entries.desc: Streamlit app: AI summaries, mindmaps, IOC/TTP extraction, and ATT&CK Navigator layers.

entries.name: aiocrioc
entries.desc: LLM + OCR IOC extraction (pulls IOCs from images/PDFs).

entries.name: ThreatIngestor
entries.desc: Extracts/aggregates IOCs from feeds; integrates with MISP/ThreatKB (pairs well with LLM post-processing).

entries.name: IATelligence
entries.desc: Explains imported Windows APIs in PE files via GPT and maps to MITRE ATT&CK.

entries.name: MCP_Security
entries.desc: MCP server (ORKL) for querying the ORKL threat-intel API.

entries.name: threat-intelligence-cti-analysis
entries.desc: NLP/LLM pipeline for IOC extraction, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, and knowledge-graph generation from unstructured CTI.

entries.name: CTI-BERT
entries.desc: BERT model pretrained from scratch on a large cybersecurity text corpus for downstream CTI extraction, classification, and question-answering tasks.

sections.title: Log Analysis / SIEM / SOC Triage
sections.intro: AI agents for SOC alert triage, investigation, and incident response.

entries.name: AI-SOC-Agent
entries.desc: Black Hat 2025 MCP server exposing security-investigation tools (ELK, IRIS).

entries.name: soctalk
entries.desc: LangGraph SOC automation agent with MCP integrations for Wazuh, Cortex, TheHive, and MISP plus mock-agent test lab.

entries.name: Vigil SOC
entries.desc: Open-source AI SOC with readable Python agents, Markdown playbooks, and MCP integrations for triage, investigation, hunting, response, reporting, and forensics.

entries.name: agentic-soc-platform
entries.desc: Agentic SOC platform (LangGraph/Dify) with local-LLM support.

entries.name: SigmAIQ
entries.desc: pySigma wrapper and LangChain toolkit for automatic Sigma rule creation and translation; LGPL-2.1 licensed.

entries.name: SOCGPT
entries.desc: LLM log summarization, severity triage, MITRE mapping, and Q&A.

entries.name: AttackGen
entries.desc: LLM-driven incident-response scenario generator using MITRE ATT&CK + ATLAS.

sections.title: Reverse Engineering
sections.intro: LLM-assisted binary analysis and traffic inspection.

entries.name: Gepetto
entries.desc: IDA Pro plugin: GPT adds comments and meaningful variable names.

entries.name: ida-pro-mcp
entries.desc: MCP bridge for IDA Pro exposing decompile, disassemble, xref, rename, and debugging workflows to LLM clients.

entries.name: GhidraMCP
entries.desc: MCP server exposing Ghidra reverse-engineering ops to any MCP-capable LLM.

entries.name: ReVa
entries.desc: Ghidra-focused reverse-engineering assistant with MCP support, Claude Skills integration, and long-form analysis workflows.

entries.name: GhidrAssistMCP
entries.desc: Native Ghidra MCP extension with broad tool coverage, headless support, and security-sensitive tool gating.

entries.name: ghidra-mcp
entries.desc: Ghidra MCP server with large tool coverage, GUI plugin, headless server, and lazy tool loading.

entries.name: GhidrOllama
entries.desc: Ghidra script using the Ollama API for function analysis/renaming.

entries.name: GhidraGPT
entries.desc: Ghidra plugin that integrates LLMs for automated code refactoring and analysis.

entries.name: LLM4Decompile
entries.desc: Research project for binary-to-C decompilation with LLMs; code is MIT, but model weights use a more restrictive license.

entries.name: x64dbg_mcp
entries.desc: MCP server exposing x64dbg debugging and reverse-engineering operations to AI clients.

entries.name: binaryninja-mcp
entries.desc: MCP server for Binary Ninja-assisted reverse engineering.

entries.name: OGhidra
entries.desc: Natural-language Ghidra analysis via Ollama.

entries.name: ghidra_tools (G-3PO)
entries.desc: Ghidra plugin for AI-assisted decompiled-code analysis.

entries.name: gpt-wpre
entries.desc: Whole-program reverse engineering with GPT-3.

entries.name: burpgpt
entries.desc: Burp Suite extension integrating GPT for passive scanning.

entries.name: Burp-extension-for-GPT
entries.desc: Burp extension to analyze HTTP traffic with GPT.

sections.title: LLM Red-Teaming & Guardrails
sections.intro: Tools for attacking and defending LLM applications themselves.

entries.name: garak
entries.desc: The LLM vulnerability scanner — probes for prompt injection, jailbreaks, data leakage, and more.

entries.name: PyRIT
entries.desc: Python Risk Identification Tool; battle-tested across 100+ GenAI red-team operations.

entries.name: promptfoo
entries.desc: LLM eval + red-teaming/pentesting CLI with 50+ attack plugins (MIT).
entries.note: *Note: OpenAI announced an acquisition agreement in March 2026; remains MIT-licensed — track governance.*

entries.name: Augustus
entries.desc: Single-binary LLM security testing framework for prompt injection, jailbreaks, and adversarial attacks across many providers.

entries.name: agentic_security
entries.desc: Agentic LLM vulnerability scanner and AI red-team kit for jailbreaks, prompt injection, fuzzing, and API stress testing.

entries.name: HackAgent
entries.desc: Python SDK and CLI for red-teaming AI agents with research-backed attacks such as AdvPrefix, AutoDAN-Turbo, PAIR, TAP, FlipAttack, BoN, and static templates across agent frameworks.
entries.note: — **note:** works locally without an API key; optional cloud reporting is available.

entries.name: HiveTrace Red
entries.desc: Early-stage LLM red-teaming framework with 80+ attack templates, async evaluation pipelines, WildGuard evaluators, multi-provider support, and HTML reporting.
entries.note: — **note:** young project with limited independent adoption signal.

entries.name: DeepTeam
entries.desc: Open-source framework for red-teaming LLMs and LLM systems across jailbreaks, prompt injection, data leakage, and safety risks.

entries.name: Moonshot
entries.desc: Modular tool for benchmarking, red-teaming, and evaluating LLM applications with custom connectors and recipes.

entries.name: Guardrails AI
entries.desc: Python framework for adding input/output guards, validators, structured-output controls, and Guardrails Hub checks to LLM applications.

entries.name: Giskard
entries.desc: Open-source evaluation, testing, and red-teaming framework for LLM agents, including agent vulnerability scanning and RAG evaluation workflows.

entries.name: LangKit
entries.desc: LLM monitoring toolkit extracting safety/security signals such as jailbreak similarity, prompt-injection similarity, hallucination checks, PII patterns, toxicity, and refusal metrics.

entries.name: LLM Guard
entries.desc: Suite of input/output scanners (PII, prompt injection, etc.).

entries.name: Rebuff
entries.desc: Archived prompt-injection detector (heuristics + LLM + vector DB + canary tokens).

entries.name: NeMo Guardrails
entries.desc: Programmable guardrails (input/output/dialog/retrieval rails) for LLM apps.

entries.name: PurpleLlama
entries.desc: Llama Guard classifiers, CodeShield, and CyberSecEval.

entries.name: LLAMATOR
entries.desc: Red-teaming framework for chatbots and GenAI systems; CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 licensed.

entries.name: Vigil
entries.desc: Library/REST API to scan prompts and responses for prompt injection.

entries.name: Counterfit
entries.desc: ML/AI penetration-testing automation tool.

entries.name: AI-Red-Teaming-Playground-Labs
entries.desc: CTFd-based AI red-team training challenges.

entries.name: EasyJailbreak
entries.desc: Framework for building and testing adversarial jailbreak prompts.

entries.name: TextAttack
entries.desc: Python framework for adversarial attacks, data augmentation, and training for NLP models; useful for robustness testing beyond chat-only LLM scanners.

entries.name: GPTFuzz
entries.desc: Research framework for red-teaming LLMs with auto-generated jailbreak prompts.

entries.name: HarmBench
entries.desc: ICML 2024 standardized evaluation framework for automated red-teaming and robust-refusal benchmarking.

entries.name: llm-attacks (GCG)
entries.desc: Canonical Greedy Coordinate Gradient adversarial-suffix attack implementation for transferable attacks on aligned language models.

entries.name: nanoGCG
entries.desc: Fast, lightweight PyTorch implementation of the GCG adversarial-suffix algorithm.

entries.name: JailbreakBench
entries.desc: NeurIPS 2024 open robustness benchmark and leaderboard for generating and defending against LLM jailbreaks.

entries.name: Open-Prompt-Injection
entries.desc: Open-source toolkit and benchmark for implementing and evaluating prompt-injection attacks, defenses, and LLM-integrated applications.

entries.name: PINT Benchmark
entries.desc: Prompt-injection test benchmark for evaluating detectors and guardrails across multilingual prompt injection, jailbreak, benign, and hard-negative inputs.

entries.name: PIArena
entries.desc: ACL 2026 toolbox and benchmark for prompt-injection attacks and defenses, with ready-to-use attacks/defenses, evaluation pipelines, agent benchmarks, a Hugging Face dataset, and leaderboard.

entries.name: Whistleblower
entries.desc: Offensive testing tool for inferring system prompts and discovering capabilities of LLM applications exposed through APIs.
entries.note: — **note:** no LICENSE file found.

entries.name: LLMmap
entries.desc: Minimal-query fingerprinting tool for identifying LLMs from behavioral traces, with a pretrained open-set inference model.

entries.name: llm-security
entries.desc: Original PoC for indirect prompt-injection attacks.

entries.name: JailbreakLLMs
entries.desc: Research dataset of 6,387 ChatGPT prompts, including in-the-wild jailbreak prompts from Reddit, Discord, websites, and open datasets.

entries.name: Do-Not-Answer
entries.desc: Dataset for evaluating LLM safeguards on unsafe or policy-sensitive prompts.

entries.name: prompt-injection-defenses
entries.desc: Curated catalog of practical defenses against prompt injection.

entries.name: Wolf Defender Prompt Injection
entries.desc: Hugging Face text-classification model for prompt-injection detection in agents, chatbots, and CI workflows.

entries.name: DeBERTa v3 Prompt Injection v2
entries.desc: Apache-licensed prompt-injection classifier usable via Transformers pipelines and ONNX.

entries.name: PromptGuard
entries.desc: ModernBERT-based prompt-injection and jailbreak classifier.

entries.name: Prompt Guard 86M
entries.desc: Meta prompt-injection and jailbreak classifier from the Llama Guard family.

entries.name: prompt-injection-sentinel
entries.desc: ModernBERT-large classifier for prompt-injection and jailbreak detection.

entries.name: SecGPT
entries.desc: Open cybersecurity-tuned LLM family for vulnerability analysis, log/traffic investigation, anomaly detection, attack/defense reasoning, command analysis, and security Q&A.

entries.name: Trendyol Cybersecurity LLM v2 70B
entries.desc: Defense-focused cybersecurity LLM based on Llama-3.3-70B, trained on an alignment-safe security instruction dataset for SOC, cloud, AppSec, detection, and vulnerability-management workflows.

entries.name: WhiteRabbitNeo 2.5 Qwen Coder 7B
entries.desc: Cybersecurity-oriented Qwen2.5-Coder fine-tune positioned for offensive and defensive security assistance.

entries.name: Lily-Cybersecurity-7B-v0.2
entries.desc: Mistral-7B-Instruct fine-tune for cybersecurity assistance, trained on hand-crafted security and hacking-related instruction pairs.

entries.name: RavenX CyberAgent 35B Q4_K_M
entries.desc: GGUF security-specialized text-generation model positioned for pentest, bug-bounty, tool-calling, MCP, CVSS/CWE, and MITRE ATT&CK workflows.
entries.note: — **note:** built from an abliterated base model and marketed for autonomous security assessment; use only in authorized, sandboxed agent harnesses with tool-call validation.

sections.title: LLM Honeypots & Deception
sections.intro: Honeypots and deception that use LLMs to simulate convincing systems.

entries.name: Beelzebub
entries.desc: Low-code honeypot using LLMs to simulate SSH/HTTP/MCP services (Go).

entries.name: DECEIVE
entries.desc: Proof-of-concept LLM-powered SSH honeypot that evaluates sessions as benign, suspicious, or malicious.

entries.name: TRAP
entries.desc: Research code for Targeted Random Adversarial Prompt honeypots that identify black-box LLM usage through model-specific prompt suffixes (ACL 2024 Findings).

entries.name: shelLM
entries.desc: LLM-powered SSH honeypot (paper *"LLM in the Shell"*).

entries.name: VelLMes
entries.desc: Multi-protocol LLM honeypot framework (successor to shelLM).

entries.name: llm-honeypot
entries.desc: Cowrie SSH honeypot extended with prompt-injection traps to detect LLM hacker agents.

sections.title: CTF / Exploit / Bug-Bounty Agents & Benchmarks
sections.intro: Offensive agents and the benchmarks used to evaluate them.

entries.name: SWE-agent (EnIGMA)
entries.desc: EnIGMA offensive-CTF mode; SOTA on NYU CTF, InterCode-CTF, and Cybench (v0.7 branch).

entries.name: Cybench
entries.desc: 40 professional CTF tasks across 4 competitions; widely used by AI safety institutes.

entries.name: NYU CTF Bench
entries.desc: Dockerized CSAW CTF challenges for LLM-agent evaluation.

entries.name: CTFTiny
entries.desc: Lightweight CTF benchmark from the NYU LLM CTF group; GPL-2.0 licensed.

entries.name: InterCode
entries.desc: Interactive-coding benchmark incl. InterCode-CTF.

entries.name: inspect_evals
entries.desc: Maintained Inspect AI evaluation suite containing multiple cyber benchmarks and tasks.

entries.name: BountyBench
entries.desc: 25 real systems / 40 bug bounties for Detect-Exploit-Patch evaluation.

entries.name: Cyber-Zero
entries.desc: Trains cybersecurity agents without runtime; ships an EnIGMA+ scaffold.

entries.name: ExploitBench
entries.desc: Measures AI-agent progress on V8/Chromium exploit ladders.

entries.name: AI Goat
entries.desc: Vulnerable-by-design local LLM CTF for learning prompt injection, insecure output handling, data leakage, excessive agency, and related LLM app risks.
entries.note: — **note:** GPL-2.0 licensed.

entries.name: Damn Vulnerable LLM Agent
entries.desc: Deliberately vulnerable LangChain ReAct agent for practicing prompt-injection and Thought/Action/Observation injection attacks.

entries.name: claude-bug-bounty
entries.desc: Claude Code plugin orchestrating recon → vuln classes → reporting.

entries.name: Bug-Bounty-Agents
entries.desc: 43 AI agent personas for Claude Code / Copilot / Cursor across the bug-bounty lifecycle.

entries.name: ai-exploits
entries.desc: Real-world AI/ML exploits (Metasploit modules + Nuclei templates) for MLflow, Ray, H2O.

sections.title: Cloud / IaC / DFIR / OSINT / Phishing
sections.intro: AI tooling for cloud/IaC security, digital forensics, OSINT, and phishing detection.

entries.name: EscalateGPT
entries.desc: GPT-based discovery of privilege-escalation paths in AWS IAM policies.

entries.name: MemoryInvestigator
entries.desc: Volatility 3 + LLM + RAG for memory-forensic triage.

entries.name: Volatility-MCP-Server
entries.desc: MCP exposing Volatility 3 plugins for natural-language memory forensics.

entries.name: llm_osint
entries.desc: Proof-of-concept LLM OSINT framework using knowledge and web agents for internet research workflows.

entries.name: ai_osint
entries.desc: Curated AI-OSINT dorks, queries, and techniques for discovering exposed LLM and AI infrastructure.

entries.name: PhishLLM
entries.desc: Reference-less phishing detection via LLM brand recognition (USENIX'24).

entries.name: mcp-dnstwist
entries.desc: MCP server for dnstwist DNS fuzzing to support typosquatting, phishing, and lookalike-domain analysis.

entries.name: osintgpt
entries.desc: OpenAI embeddings + Qdrant over OSINT corpora.

entries.name: gpt-osint
entries.desc: Web-based GPT-4 OSINT tool over social-media dumps and CSVs.

awesome.name: awesome-llm-cybersecurity-tools
awesome.desc: Tenable's list (archived but a strong reference).

awesome.name: awesome-ai-security
awesome.desc: For pentesters, bug hunters, and researchers.

awesome.name: awesome-cybersecurity-agentic-ai
awesome.desc: Agentic-AI cybersecurity tools and security MCP servers.

awesome.name: open-source-llm-scanners
awesome.desc: Open-source LLM scanners and testing tools.

awesome.name: awesome-mcp-security
awesome.desc: MCP security resources, tools, writeups, and server/client risk references.

awesome.name: awesome-ml-security
awesome.desc: Trail of Bits' curated machine-learning security resources.

awesome.name: awesome-ml-for-cybersecurity
awesome.desc: Large classic list of machine-learning-for-cybersecurity resources (stale-ish but still useful).

awesome.name: Awesome-AI4DevSecOps
awesome.desc: Taxonomy of AI-driven security solutions for DevSecOps.

awesome.name: awesome-threat-intelligence
awesome.desc: Classic CTI list (pairs with the AI-CTI section).
